news.headline: Omega Flex, Inc. declares regular quarterly dividend of $0.34 per share for Q4 2024, payable on January 7, 2025.
news.summary: On December 5, 2024, Omega Flex, Inc. (NASDAQ: OFLX) based in Exton, Pennsylvania, announced a regular quarterly dividend of $0.34 per share. $7 trillion electrification boom driven by AI power demand projected as U.S. power demand surges at 2.5% annually. New Tema Electrification ETF (NYSE:VOLT) introduced as world's first fund dedicated to modern technology infrastructure.
news.summary: AI investing is commonly associated with companies like Nvidia and Microsoft. However, the increasing power demand to support AI innovation is a less recognized aspect of this industry. The U.S. power demand is projected to rise by 2.5% annually, leading to a potential $7 trillion investment in infrastructure overhaul. This surge is driven by the need to sustain AI breakthroughs, digital asset mining, and next-generation mobility innovations. Tema ETFs recently launched the Tema Electrification ETF (NYSE:VOLT), the world's first fund dedicated to the infrastructure powering modern technology. Factors contributing to the rise in power demand include energy-hungry AI data centers, which consume seven times more power than traditional facilities, and the need for upgrading aging infrastructure. Approximately 70% of U.S. transmission lines and power transformers are over 25 years old, highlighting the urgent need for investment in the power grid.

news.headline: DocuSign Inc (NASDAQ: DOCU) reported Q3 revenue of $754.8M, beating estimates. Stock soared on earnings beat.
news.summary: Cloud company DocuSign Inc (NASDAQ:DOCU) released their Q3 financial results, surpassing expectations. They reported a revenue of $754.8 million, higher than the estimated $745.26 million. Their adjusted earnings of 90 cents per share also exceeded analyst predictions of 87 cents per share. Total revenue saw an 8% increase from the previous year, with subscription revenue up by 8% to $734.7 million and Professional services and other revenue rising by 11% to $20.1 million. news.headline: GitLab's stock surges post strong Q3 results: 23 cents EPS (beat est. - 16 cents) and $196M revenue (beat est. - $187.93M).ARR customers: over $5K - 9,519 (+16% YoY).
news.summary: GitLab Inc. (GTLB) released its Q3 results, exceeding analyst expectations. Earnings were reported at 23 cents per share, beating the estimated 16 cents. Quarterly revenue stood at $196 million, surpassing the projected $187.93 million and showing growth from $149.67 million in the same period last year. The company saw a 16% increase in customers with over $5,000 of ARR, totaling 9,519. Additionally, customers with over $100,000 of ARR reached a significant number. More details are available through Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Rubrik Inc (NYSE:RBRK) reports Q3 revenue of $236.2 million, beats estimates. Total revenue up 43% yoy, ARR at $1.002 billion.
news.summary: Rubrik Inc (NYSE:RBRK) released its Q3 financial results, exceeding expectations with a revenue of $236.2 million compared to estimated $217.52 million. The adjusted EPS was a loss of 21 cents, better than the estimated loss of 40 cents. Total revenue saw a 43% increase year-over-year, with annual recurring revenue (ARR) going up by 38% to $1.002 billion. Rubrik gained 2,085 customers with $100,000 or more in subscription ARR, a 32% increase from the previous year. The company generated $23.1 million in cash flow from operations and $15.6 million in free cash flow.

news.headline: Asana (NYSE:ASAN) shares soar after Q3 results; losses at 2 cents per share, revenue hits $183.88M. Core customers up by 11% YoY.
news.summary: Asana, Inc. (NYSE:ASAN) released their third-quarter financial results post Thursday's market close. In this report, Asana disclosed quarterly losses amounting to two cents per share, surpassing analysts' expectations of a seven cents loss per share. Revenue for the quarter reached $183.88 million, exceeding the anticipated $180.66 million and showcasing growth from last year's $166.5 million. The number of Core customers, defined as those spending $5,000 or more annually, rose to 23,609, marking an 11% increase compared to the previous year.

news.headline: National Beverage Corp. (NASDAQ: FIZZ) reported Q2 net sales of $291M, operating income of $58M, and EPS of $.49 - 7% growth. YTD net sales reached $621M, with $127M in operating income - significant growth.
news.summary: National Beverage Corp. (NASDAQ: FIZZ) reported its financial results for the second quarter and six-month periods ending October 26, 2024. In the second quarter, net sales reached $291 million, with a gross margin of 38% of net sales. Operating income saw a 7% increase to $58 million, while earnings per share rose to $.49 from $.47. Over the six-month period, net sales totaled $621 million, and operating income grew by $10 million to $127 million. Net income also experienced a 1% increase compared to the previous year.

news.headline: Athabasca Oil Corp plans to focus on cash flow per share growth in their 2025 budget, directing 100% of free cash flow to share buybacks.
news.summary: In Calgary, Alberta, on December 5, 2024, Athabasca Oil Corporation (TSX: ATH) revealed its 2025 budget, outlining capital projects aimed at maintaining a blend of cash flow expansion and sustaining a reliable capital return strategy. news.headline: Chairwoman of Ionic Digital, Elizabeth LaPuma, announces resignation of CEO and CFO John Penver in Austin, Texas. Senior leadership assumes day-to-day operations.
news.summary: In Austin, Texas, Elizabeth LaPuma, Chairwoman of the Board and Director of Ionic Digital, Inc., a digital infrastructure and cryptocurrency mining company, disclosed the voluntary departure of Interim CEO and CFO, John Penver. news.headline: Genesis Energy, L.P. has raised its Tender Offer for 8.0% Senior Notes due 2027 from $385 million to $575 million.
news.summary: Houston-based Genesis Energy, L.P. (NYSE: GEL) revealed an update on their cash tender offer for 8.0% Senior Notes due 2027. Initially capped at $385 million, the maximum aggregate principal amount for purchase in the Tender Offer has been raised to $575 million. This amendment, referred to as the "Tender Offer Cap," was made in accordance with the offer to purchase dated December 5, 2024.
